He draws a matchup against the Utah Jazz, who on paper have the rim protection to slow his turbo-charged downhill game, but it’s fair to question how much those defenders will actually see the floor. Utah is coming off another win and is playing on the second night of a back-to-back. Do I expect them to deploy disciplined, game-specific coverages to consistently stop Avdija’s downhill attack? I don’t.

Points Scored LeBron James o22.5 Points Scored (-112) Best Odds o23.5 -120 Pick made: 10 hours ago Jon Metler Betting Analyst Analysis o24.5 -109 o24.5 -110 o24.5 -102 o23.5 -120 o24.5 -129 With Luka Dončić sidelined, LeBron James has every opportunity to put on a show against the Dallas Mavericks. His points prop is sitting at -112 for the over 22.5, and that’s a number worth attacking given that I price it much closer to -175. Daniel Gafford provides solid rim protection, but he’s typically limited to around 20 minutes per game. During the stretches when he’s off the floor, Dallas lacks a true back-line defender who can consistently disrupt LeBron’s downhill attacks. The Mavericks also struggle with communication and transition defense. Even at this stage of his career, LeBron remains elite at running the floor and capitalizing on early scoring opportunities. With his basketball IQ and ability to exploit defensive lapses, he should be able to pick apart this version of the Mavericks’ defense.
